Marketing.title: How To Use AI To Support Your Design Process
Marketing.summary: If you’re trying to figure out how to use AI for graphic design without wrecking your creative process, you’re asking the right question.  This is about removing friction, not replacing designers. AI tools now live inside Figma, Adobe, and most product workflows.

Marketing.title: How Voice Search Ads Are Changing The Search Term Report in 2026
Marketing.summary: If you’ve looked at a Google Ads Search Term Report (STR) lately, you know it feels like stepping off a merry-go-round a little too fast. The tidy lists of “best running shoes” or “mortgage rates NYC” that we built entire careers around? Those are fading out.

Marketing.title: The Definitive Guide to Online Reputation Management
Marketing.summary: Online reputation management (ORM) is the practice of monitoring and improving (when necessary) how your brand is perceived on the internet. In practice, this means responding to negative reviews, suppressing negative search results or comments, and addressing any misinformation about your company.

Design.summary: Design is about pacing and feelings as much as pixels and patterns. Design.title: Moving From Moment.js To The JS Temporal API
Design.summary: The way JavaScript handles time has evolved significantly, from the built-in `Date` API to Moment.js and now Temporal. The new standard fills gaps in the original `Date` API while addressing limitations found in Moment and other libraries. Joe Attardi shares practical “recipes” for migrating Moment-

Design.title: Beyond `border-radius`: What The CSS `corner-shape` Property Unlocks For Everyday UI
Design.summary: For years, developers have been hacking around the limitations of `border-radius`, using clip-path, SVG masks, and fragile workarounds just to get anything other than round corners. The new `corner-shape` property finally changes that, opening the door to beveled, scooped, and squircle corners.
